title: Fix PageFrameGrid to show fixed-size frame grid with empty slots instead of growing list
description: page-replacement.ts uses frames.filter(f => f !== null) at lines 79,94,106 which produces a GROWING frame list during the loading phase. A 3-frame system should always show 3 cells, with empty ones marked as '-'. The current visual implies the number of frames changes, which is a teaching lie. Fix: include null frames in the event, render them as empty cells in PageFrameGrid.
acceptanceCriteria: PageFrameGrid always shows exactly frameCount cells, regardless of how many are loaded
Empty frames render as '-' or empty cell with distinct styling
The grid size never changes during step-through — only contents change
Visual clearly distinguishes: empty frame (not yet used) vs occupied frame

title: Replace SJF ready.sort() with ready.reduce() to better represent 'find minimum' algorithm logic
description: scheduling.ts:263 uses ready.sort((a,b) => a.remainingTime - b.remainingTime)[0] to find the shortest job. This teaches students that SJF SORTS all processes every tick, when the actual algorithm just finds the MINIMUM. Using ready.reduce((min, p) => p.remainingTime < min.remainingTime ? p : min) better represents the algorithm's logic and is O(n) instead of O(n log n).
acceptanceCriteria: SJF uses reduce/find to locate minimum remaining time process
Priority scheduling uses the same pattern for minimum priority
No change in output — same process is selected
Step description updated: 'SJF selects P2 because it has the shortest remaining time (3) among ready processes'

title: Add pseudocode display for all 14+ OS algorithms matching CLRS/Silberschatz notation
description: Zero algorithms have pseudocode (I6 score: 0/3 across all). Add CLRS-style pseudocode strings to each algorithm's config/metadata. Pseudocode must map line-by-line to the actual implementation so students can trace both simultaneously. Use Silberschatz notation for Banker's, OSTEP notation for scheduling.
acceptanceCriteria: Every algorithm has a pseudocode string in a displayable format
Pseudocode uses standard textbook notation (1-indexed, CAPITALIZE keywords)
Line numbers in pseudocode can be mapped to implementation lines
Pseudocode panel visible alongside visualization during step-through
Current pseudocode line highlights during step-through

title: Add inline educational comments to all engine files — 'why' at every decision point
description: Engine comments currently score 1-2/3 on I10. They describe WHAT the code does but rarely WHY. Add educational comments at every algorithmic decision point. Example in scheduling.ts FCFS: '// FCFS rule: pick the process that arrived earliest. This is non-preemptive — once selected, it runs to completion.' Example in page-replacement.ts LRU: '// Temporal locality: recently used pages are likely to be used again. LRU evicts the page accessed LONGEST AGO.'
acceptanceCriteria: Every algorithm function has a 2-3 line educational comment explaining its core principle
Every if/else decision point has a comment explaining WHY the branch is taken
Every event.push has a comment explaining what teaching moment this step represents
Reading JUST the comments teaches the algorithm (narrative test)

title: Rename single-letter variables in scheduling.ts for teaching readability
description: scheduling.ts uses `p` for process (26 occurrences), `t` for tick, `i`/`j` for loop indices. For teaching code, every variable should be self-documenting. Rename: p→currentProcess, t→currentTick, running→currentlyRunning, pick→selectedProcess. This makes the code readable WITHOUT comments.
acceptanceCriteria: No single-letter variables remain except loop index 'i' in non-critical loops
Every variable name describes its purpose: currentProcess, selectedProcess, readyProcesses
Code reads like English: 'if (currentProcess.remainingTime === 0) { completed.add(currentProcess.id) }'
No functional change — same algorithm, same output

title: Add SRTF variant to SJF dropdown and Hoare partition analogy to Priority
description: SJF only shows non-preemptive mode in UI (I2 score: 1/3). The preemptive variant (SRTF) is implemented but hidden. Add: SRTF as a dropdown option alongside SJF, with explanation of the difference. For Priority scheduling, expose the preemptive toggle. Each variant should explain what changes and why.
acceptanceCriteria: SJF dropdown shows 'SJF (Non-preemptive)' and 'SRTF (Preemptive)' options
Priority dropdown shows 'Priority (Non-preemptive)' and 'Priority (Preemptive)' options
Step descriptions explain the variant: 'SRTF preempts P1 because newly arrived P3 has shorter remaining time'
Compare All includes both variants

title: Add NRU and Working Set page replacement variants
description: We have 4 page replacement algorithms (FIFO, LRU, Optimal, Clock). Missing important variants: NRU (Not Recently Used — uses reference+modify bits), Working Set (evicts pages outside the working set window). These are covered in OSTEP Ch.22 and Tanenbaum. Adding them deepens the module beyond competitors.
acceptanceCriteria: NRU page replacement algorithm added with class-based eviction (reference bit + modify bit)
Working Set page replacement with configurable window parameter (Δ)
Both included in Compare All mode
Step descriptions explain the eviction logic for each new algorithm

title: Fix ResourceAllocationGraph cycle highlighting to trace actual cycle edges, not just node membership
description: OSModule.tsx:970,983 checks inCycle = deadlockedSet.has(pid) && cycleSet.has(pid). This highlights nodes that are in BOTH sets but doesn't trace the actual cycle path through edges. An edge from P0→R0 might be in the cycle but isn't highlighted if the logic only checks node-level membership. Fix: trace the cycle array to highlight specific edges.
acceptanceCriteria: Cycle edges are highlighted by tracing the cycle path: P0→R0→P1→R1→P0
Non-cycle edges from deadlocked processes remain un-highlighted
The visual clearly shows the circular dependency chain
Cycle arrow animation (pulsing or flowing) to draw attention

title: Add color legends to GanttChart, PageFrameGrid, and Memory Translation panels
description: V4 Legend scores 0-1 for most visualizers. GanttChart has NO inline legend (process colors only in properties panel). PageFrameGrid has HIT/FAULT labels but no color legend. Memory Translation has status badges but no legend. Add compact inline legends below each visualizer.
acceptanceCriteria: GanttChart: process color legend directly below the chart
PageFrameGrid: legend showing green=HIT, red=FAULT, highlight=current step
Memory Translation: legend showing green=TLB HIT, blue=PT HIT, red=PAGE FAULT
ThreadSyncCanvas legend already exists (good) — verify it's readable (currently 8px)
All legends use minimum 10px text

title: Add Python display code for top 5 algorithms alongside TypeScript engine
description: I7 Multi-Language scores 1/3 — only TypeScript engine code exists. Python is the #1 teaching language for OS concepts (OSTEP homework simulators are all Python). Add a toggleable code panel showing clean Python implementations that match the TypeScript engine logic, without framework-specific event generation.
acceptanceCriteria: Python implementations for: FCFS, Round Robin, FIFO Page Replacement, Banker's Algorithm, Virtual Memory Translation
Python code is clean, standalone, and uses descriptive variable names
Code panel toggleable in the UI (TypeScript | Python)
Python code matches the algorithm logic line-by-line with TypeScript
Comments in Python code are educational (not restating code)

title: Add 'Textbook vs Production' comparison for scheduling and page replacement
description: I9 Real-World Implementation scores 0/3. Students never see how textbook algorithms relate to production code. Add comparison panels: (1) MLFQ textbook vs Linux CFS (virtual runtime, red-black tree), (2) LRU textbook vs Redis approximate LRU (random sampling), (3) First Fit textbook vs Linux Buddy System. Show key differences and why production versions diverge.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 3 'Textbook vs Production' comparison panels in Learn tab
MLFQ vs CFS: show virtual runtime concept, red-black tree, no explicit priority queues
LRU vs Redis approx LRU: show random sampling approach, why exact LRU is too expensive
First Fit vs Buddy System: show power-of-2 splitting, why it's faster for page frames
Each comparison explains WHY production diverges from textbook

title: Add operation counters to all algorithm visualizations
description: No algorithm shows operation counts during execution. Add visible counters: scheduling (comparisons, context switches), page replacement (page faults, hits, evictions), memory (TLB hits, TLB misses, page faults), allocation (scans, splits, merges), sync (acquires, blocks, releases). These counters make complexity VISIBLE.
acceptanceCriteria: Real-time operation counter visible during step-through for all concepts
Scheduling: context switch count, idle ticks, total comparisons
Page Replacement: running fault count, hit count, eviction count
Memory: TLB hit rate updating per step, page fault rate
Counters reset on new simulation run
Counters shown in comparison mode for each algorithm

title: Add 'Problem Before Solution' mode — show broken behavior first, then the fix
description: OSTEP's gold standard teaching pattern: show what goes WRONG before the fix. Apply: (1) Thread Sync: race condition demo corrupting shared counter, THEN mutex fix. (2) Scheduling: Convoy Effect in FCFS, THEN SJF fix. (3) Page replacement: thrashing scenario. Source: OSTEP -c flag pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Thread Sync: 'Race Condition Demo' button showing broken concurrent counter increment
CPU Scheduling: 'Convoy Effect Demo' button showing one long job blocking short ones
Each problem demo followed by 'Now try [algorithm] on the same input' prompt
Pattern matches Belady's Anomaly approach (our highest-rated content)

title: Consolidate duplicate MLFQ implementations — keep mlfq-scheduler.ts, deprecate scheduling.ts mlfq()
description: Two MLFQ implementations overlap: scheduling.ts:702-867 (simpler, has event type bug) and mlfq-scheduler.ts:150-461 (full, with I/O boost). The simpler one has confirmed 'preempt' instead of 'demote' bug. Keep mlfq-scheduler.ts as single source, wire compareAlgorithms() to use it.
acceptanceCriteria: compareAlgorithms() uses mlfqScheduler from mlfq-scheduler.ts
scheduling.ts mlfq() function removed or marked @deprecated
All MLFQ events use correct 'demote' type

title: Add compaction operation to memory allocation simulation
description: MemoryAllocStep type includes 'compact' as action (memory-alloc.ts:23) but no compaction function exists. Compaction eliminates external fragmentation. Add Compact button that relocates all allocated blocks contiguously.
acceptanceCriteria: Compact button in memory allocation controls
Compaction moves allocated blocks to be contiguous from address 0
Fragmentation drops to 0% after compaction
Animation shows blocks sliding left to close gaps
